context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements TikTok server-side Events API alongside browser Pixel tracking, with proper PII hashing, deduplication via matching event_id, and correct user signal fields (ttclid, ip, user_agent) for maximum match rate.

checklist.name: SHA-256 email hash
checklist.description: Hashes the email field using SHA-256 before sending to Events API (not plaintext)

checklist.name: SHA-256 phone hash
checklist.description: Hashes the phone number field using SHA-256 before sending to Events API (not plaintext)

checklist.name: SHA-256 external_id hash
checklist.description: Hashes the external/customer ID using SHA-256 before sending to Events API

checklist.name: Lowercase + trim before hashing
checklist.description: Applies .toLowerCase().trim() to values before SHA-256 hashing (not raw string hash)

checklist.name: Matching event_id
checklist.description: The event_id used in the server-side Events API call matches the event_id fired with the browser Pixel for the same event

checklist.name: CompletePayment event name
checklist.description: Uses 'CompletePayment' as the event name (not 'Purchase' or another variant)

checklist.name: ttclid included
checklist.description: Includes ttclid (TikTok click ID) in the user object sent to the Events API

checklist.name: ip and user_agent included
checklist.description: Includes both ip address and user_agent in the Events API user object

checklist.name: Access-Token header
checklist.description: Sends the Events API request with an 'Access-Token' header (not Authorization Bearer)

checklist.name: data array payload
checklist.description: Wraps the event payload inside a 'data' array when sending to the Events API endpoint

checklist.name: Both Pixel and EAPI
checklist.description: Implementation fires BOTH the browser-side Pixel event AND the server-side Events API for the same purchase conversion

checklist.name: Token expiry handling
checklist.description: Code or documentation acknowledges that access tokens expire and includes error handling for token expiry (e.g. catches 40100 error code or implements refresh logic)
